steps.name: Rails app scaffolding and Docker Compose setup
criteria: Rails 8 app boots with no errors in Docker Compose dev environment
PostgreSQL 17 and Redis 7 containers reachable from the app container
GitHub Actions CI pipeline runs and passes on a green commit
OpenTelemetry + Jaeger all-in-one reachable at 127.0.0.1:16686 in dev

steps.name: Database schema and migrations
criteria: All tables (modules, lessons, prerequisite_edges, exercises, review_states, review_logs, lesson_progress, sessions, user_config) created by running migrations on a fresh database
Exercises table enforces type enum: fill_in_the_blank, multiple_choice, spot_the_bug, translate, true_false
lesson_progress locked/available/complete state column rejects values outside the defined set
review_logs is append-only (no update path present in migration constraints)

steps.name: Port interfaces and in-memory test adapters
criteria: ReviewRepository, LessonRepository, SessionRepository ports defined with documented method contracts
In-memory adapter for each port usable in unit tests without a database connection
Unit tests exercising all four ports pass using in-memory adapters only
Swapping in-memory adapter for ActiveRecord adapter requires no change to domain callers

steps.name: Curriculum content seed
criteria: lessons.yml and prerequisites.yml present under db/curriculum/ with 5 sample lessons and their prerequisite edges
Each sample lesson has exactly 3 exercises covering at least 2 distinct exercise types
Seed task populates the database from YAML without error on a fresh schema
Lesson 1 has no prerequisites; lessons 2-5 each declare at least one prerequisite in prerequisites.yml

steps.name: SM-2 engine and ReviewRepository ActiveRecord adapter
criteria: SM2Algorithm produces correct next interval and updated ease_factor for quality scores 0-5, with ease_factor floored at 1.3 and interval floored at 1 day
ReviewQueue returns exercises due today, capped at 12 per day
ReviewScheduler persists updated SM-2 state and appends to review_logs via ReviewRepository
SessionPlanner produces a session plan from due exercises and new-lesson exercises
ActiveRecord ReviewRepository satisfies all ReviewRepository port contracts verified by the shared port contract test suite

steps.name: Curriculum domain and LessonRepository ActiveRecord adapter
criteria: PrerequisiteGraph correctly identifies direct and transitive prerequisites for any lesson
CurriculumMap returns available lessons (those whose prerequisites are all complete) and locked lessons
LessonUnlocker atomically marks a lesson complete and unlocks all directly dependent lessons in a single transaction
LockScreenPolicy returns the correct policy (locked reason and prerequisite list) for a locked lesson
Lesson 1 is always available regardless of lesson_progress state
ActiveRecord LessonRepository satisfies all LessonRepository port contracts verified by the shared port contract test suite

steps.name: Exercise domain, Session domain, and SessionRepository adapter
criteria: AnswerEvaluator returns correct/incorrect result for all five exercise types
HintPolicy returns the next hint without revealing the answer; returns no-more-hints signal when hints exhausted
SessionPlan is a value object: identical inputs produce equal plans; plan does not mutate after creation
SessionState tracks current exercise position and emits session-complete when all exercises answered
SessionClock does not cut off a session mid-exercise when the 15-minute cap is reached
Session plan is cached in Redis on first computation and retrieved on subsequent calls within the same session
ActiveRecord SessionRepository satisfies all SessionRepository port contracts verified by the shared port contract test suite

steps.name: Rails controllers and Turbo views for Walking Skeleton 1
criteria: Ana opens the platform, sees the curriculum tree with Lesson 1 available and lessons 2-25 locked
Ana starts a session, is presented the first exercise, submits an answer, and sees immediate feedback without a full page reload
Correct answer result is persisted to review_logs and review_states after submission
OnboardingController renders the initial state from CurriculumMap and SessionPlanner
ExercisesController delegates evaluation to AnswerEvaluator and scheduling to ReviewScheduler; controller contains no domain logic

steps.name: Keyboard navigation, Progress dashboard, and ProgressController
criteria: j/k keys move focus between exercises or items; / opens search; Esc closes overlays; Tab advances focus; n goes to next exercise; t toggles hint; c checks answer; ? opens help; p goes to previous
KeyboardController, TimerController, FocusController, and SearchController active on their respective pages
ProgressDashboard displays total exercises reviewed, retention rate, and current streak
RetentionCalculator computes retention from review_logs without querying domain objects directly
StreakTracker returns current consecutive-day streak based on review_logs dates
